question.content: The plot shows ln(S) versus 1/T for NaIO3-H2O with linear fit y=6.266-2088x. Use ln(S)=-(DeltaH/R)*(1/T)+constant to compute DeltaH. Output exactly one decimal number with 2 decimal places, plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no percent sign, no fraction, no equation form, and no extra text. Answer format: a decimal number rounded to two decimal places; write only the numerical value itself, with no units, explanatory text, or equals sign.

step_1.content: Method: two-point van't Hoff elimination method. Pick two points on the fitted line so the intercept term is canceled.
step_1.key_point: Use Δln(S)/Δ(1/T) to remove the constant term.

step_3.content: From y = 6.266 - 2088x, subtract the two-point equations to get Δln(S) = -2088Δ(1/T), so the finite-difference slope is m = -2088.

step_4.content: Use m = -ΔH/R, then ΔH = -mR = 2088 × 8.314e-3 kJ/mol = 17.360..., which rounds to two decimals.
